commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh And king Solomon made two hundred large shields of beaten gold: six hundred shekels of gold went to one large shield. Hebrew tsinnu is rather indeterminate, denoting something sharp or pointed; "a dart "(Calmet) Paralipomenon spears. Some of the shields were made with a point, projecting from the middle, (Haydock) with which the enemy might be wounded. (Menochius) These arms were used when the king went to the temple, and were reposited in the arsenal, at his return, chap.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
